data.current_description: Render an asset from a template id and optional merge fields. Merge fields can be used to replace placeholder variables within the [Edit](#tocs_edit).
data.summary_suggestion: Render video, image, or audio from template
data.description_suggestion: Render an asset (video, image, or audio) from a saved template by providing the template id and optional merge fields to replace placeholder variables. Merge fields allow dynamic content substitution within the template's Edit configuration. Returns a queued render response with a render id that can be used to track rendering progress and retrieve the generated asset.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about what types of assets can be rendered, what the operation returns, and how to track the render. Added specificity about asset types, merge field functionality, and return value for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: The Serve API is used to interact with, and delete hosted assets including videos, images, audio files, thumbnails and poster images. Use this endpoint to fetch an asset by asset id. Note that an asset id is unique for each asset and different from the render id.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a hosted asset (video, image, audio, thumbnail, or poster) from the Serve API by its unique asset id. Each asset generated by a render has a distinct asset id that differs from the render id. This endpoint returns the asset metadata and hosting information.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description contains unnecessary context about the Serve API's general purpose and deletion capabilities, which are not relevant to this GET operation. Returns confirmation of the transfer with destination URLs and hosting status.

message: Operation 'getUploadSignedUrl' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Current summary 'Direct Upload' is vague and doesn't clarify this is a two-step process. Description is overly verbose with implementation details and cURL examples that obscure the core operation. Improved summary to be action-oriented. Restructured description to clearly explain: (1) what this operation does (request signed URL), (2) what it returns (temporary signed URL), (3) the two-step workflow, (4) supported file types, (5) the downstream use case, and (6) SDK limitations. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description:
Request a signed URL to upload a file to. The response returns a signed URL that you use to upload the file to.
The signed URL looks similar to:

https://shotstack-ingest-api-stage-sources.s3.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/5ca6hu7s9k/zzytey4v-32km-kq1z-aftr-3kcuqi0brad2/source?AWSAccessKeyId=ASIAWJV7UWDMGTZLHTXP&Expires=1677209777&Signature=PKR4dGDDdOuMTAQmDASzLGmLOeo%3D&x-amz-acl=public-read&x-amz-security-token=IQoJb3JpZ2luX2VjEGMaDmFwLX......56osBGByztm7WZdbmXzO09KR

In a separate API call, use this signed URL to send a PUT request with the binary file. Using  cURL you can use
a command like:
    
`curl -X PUT -T video.mp4 {data.attributes.url}`
    
Where **video.mp4** is the file you want to upload and **{data.attributes.url}** is the signed URL returned in
the response. The request must be a PUT type.

The SDK does not currently support the PUT request. You can use the SDK to make the request for the signed URL
and then use cURL to make the PUT request.
data.summary_suggestion: Request signed URL for file upload
data.description_suggestion: Request a temporary signed AWS S3 URL for uploading a source file. Returns a signed URL valid for a limited time that accepts PUT requests with binary file data. After obtaining the URL, make a separate PUT request with your file (video, image, or audio) to complete the upload. The uploaded file will be ingested and made available for use with the Edit API. Note: SDK does not support PUT requests; use cURL or HTTP client for the actual file upload.
data.reasoning: Current summary 'Direct Upload' is vague and doesn't clarify this is a two-step process. Description is overly verbose with implementation details and cURL examples that obscure the core operation. Improved summary to be action-oriented. Restructured description to clearly explain: (1) what this operation does (request signed URL), (2) what it returns (temporary signed URL), (3) the two-step workflow, (4) supported file types, (5) the downstream use case, and (6) SDK limitations. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description:
Generate an asset using a Shotstack or third party provider. Chose the provider, type of asset and provide prompts,
inputs and options to generate the asset. Once generated the asset can be used with the [Edit](#tocs_edit) API or
downloaded.
data.description_suggestion: Generate media assets (images, videos, voice overs, or text) using Shotstack's native AI or third-party providers (HeyGen, ElevenLabs, D-ID). Specify the provider, asset type, and provide prompts or inputs to customize generation. Returns a queued response with an asset ID for tracking. The generated asset becomes available for download or use with the Edit API once processing completes. Check status using the Get Generated Asset endpoint.
